name: Kealia Trail
tagline: The best-kept-secret ridge trail on the North Shore — wide open views from Dillingham to the Waianae Mountains.
parking: Free dirt pulloff on Farrington Highway past Dillingham Airfield — look for the trail marker on the mauka side
bestTime: Early morning — zero shade on this trail so you'll bake by 10am
talkStory: About 6 miles round trip with 2,000 ft elevation gain along a fully exposed ridge — bring at least 2 liters of water per person. The trail follows an old access road that switchbacks up the Waianae Range with insane views of the North Shore and Dillingham Airfield (you'll see skydivers below you). It connects to the Kuaokala Trail at the top for a longer adventure. One of the best sunset-facing ridge hikes if you time it right. Windy at the top — always.
